I know what you're about to say, but give up on that notion. There's no reversing METAmorphosis.
...And even if it does somehow become possible, I've already accepted the reality of this body, and of this power.
I understand.
One last thing. I've had some time to review what I know with what I learned from Zuikaku. All these phenomena we've been experiencing likely tie back to Arbiter IX: The Hermit.
If we go after U-556, we will likely be delivering ourselves straight to her.
An Arbiter? We hardly have the resources to deal with their spare vessels, let alone a complete Arbiter.
But, there's no other way. We have to keep searching the tower.
Zuikaku, I'm sorry for getting you tangled up in this mess.
Oh, uh, don't be sorry!
You've given me the VIP treatment while I was at the Floating Fortress, so it's only natural I return the favor!
Whatever happens, I'm with you 'til the end!
Thank you. Everyone, let's bring U-556 back!

Multilayered Space.say: Mirror Sea - α phase
In a burst of light, the Tower of Midgard sprung to life again without warning.
...What just happened? Did we get teleported?
Seems so. By the looks of things, we got ejected from the tower, but are still inside the same Mirror Sea.
Yeah, I can still see the Tower of Midgard in the distance. We just have to make our way back there again...
Everyone surveyed their surroundings. The scenery around them seemed slightly different, but without a doubt, they were still inside the Mirror Sea.
Whoever is behind this clearly doesn't plan on letting us leave.
Also, take a look around. The boundary of the red sea is closing in on us.
Remember how far away it seemed when we first arrived? It's almost touching the edges of those floating cubes now.
Jade, launch your planes and take a closer look at what's going on.
You got it!
Bismarck, is it just me, or does it feel like something major shifted inside this Mirror Sea at some point?
I noticed it too. It was likely at the moment the Tower of Midgard was last activated.
Let me get one thing straight then – Zuikaku, you and Gneisenau were the first to arrive, right?
Yeah, we were looking for Ulrich when we first arrived, and there was no red sea here at all.
Oh, uh, you were sleepwalking at the time, so it's natural you wouldn't remember it.
While I can't be 100% sure, I think Jade was the first to observe the red sea.
(All I did remember from my episode is going through the gate and heading straight for the control center... No, wait, there's one more thing.)
Those giant cubes. I don't remember them being there.
Huh? That's weird. Gneisenau and I definitely saw them when we first got here.
If what Ulrich said is true, and the cubes aren't based on some kind of timetable, we must've done something to cause them to appear.
Let's see... We went through the gate, used the dome projector in the control center, and fought a bunch of mass-produced ships...
It could be anything, for all we know.
Out of those, I think passing through the gate is the most likely culprit.
The most conspicuous changes to this area occurred each time the Tower of Midgard was activated.
After I arrived, a column of light appeared from the Tower of Midgard on this side. After Zuikaku arrived, the cubes showed up along with the red sea.
And following that line of reasoning, this last activation of the tower is what caused the red sea to start expanding.
This is completely unlike any functionality of the Tower of Midgard we're familiar with in the real world, but the more pressing question is – what do those floating cubes do?
All that said, we should try to avoid the cubes as well as the red sea as much as possible. They reek of corruption to me.
Agreed. While my Geryon is able to offer some protection, I do not want to test its limits with your lives on the line.
Oh! My recon planes have spotted something!
There are recent signs of battle, and the wreckage of a Siren fleet!
Before you ask – no, they're not the same ones we fought earlier.
Don't you think that's odd? Maybe there's someone else trapped in here with us?
(Based on what we know about the Tower of Midgard, the gate allows travel between branches.)
(It shouldn't function like a fancy transference device that simply teleports you around in the same area.)
(Wait... Objects appearing out of nowhere, Sirens materializing from hidden spaces... What if we actually ARE moving within branches, except contained in a multi-layered system?)
Jade, where are the Siren wreckages? Can you lead us to them?
Of course! They're to the northeast. Interestingly,the signs of battle intensify closer to the tower.
Hang on! I just saw a bomb go off!
Someone is fighting the Sirens as we speak!
It might be U-556. Everyone, full speed towards the tower!

Incoming Enforcers.say: Mirror Sea - α phase
As the fleet got closer, it became increasingly clear who was wreaking havoc on the Sirens.
KABOOOM!
Hahaha! An army of trash is still a bunch of trash!
The Parzival of the Seas still has important things to do, so stop wasting my time!
KABOOOM!
Fine! If you're gonna be like that, I'll sink you all!
You're all going to sink!
The once cheerful and easygoing self-proclaimed knight was now ripping through the Sirens, shouting uncharacteristically.
She was so absorbed in fighting that she didn't even notice Bismarck and the rest of the fleet approaching.
Is that... U-556?
Looks sort of like her, but she sure doesn't SOUND like her.
Without a doubt, that's a telltale sign of METAmorphosis.
While her appearance is broadly the same, her rigging looks very different. It's a reflection of the changes to her psyche.
Can you use your Geryon to bring her back to normal?
I'm not sure. Geryon is able to absorb external sources of corrosion. If her hull has already been corrupted, I'm afraid there's not much I can do about that.
Does that mean... she's beyond salvation?
I'd have to examine her more closely to answer that.
(From what Scharnhorst and Gneisenau have told me, the symptoms of METAmorphosis, namely changes in power and external appearance, do not necessarily reflect the degree of corrosion.)
(In some cases, even minor hull corrosion can cause extremely noticeable abnormalities, whereas on the other hand, there are cases in which late-stage corrosion hardly exhibits any symptoms.)
(In other words, it all depends on the hull in question.)
To the best of my knowledge, U-556's condition was stable until it was exacerbated by the black mist. If I can draw it away from her the same way I did with Ulrich...
There's a chance she can still be saved.
So, don't give up hope.
Okay. Thank goodness.
Bad news, Bismarck. Before we can talk about any of that stuff, we have to first make sure she comes back in one piece.
More Sirens are coming, but this time, they've brought Enforcers.
Enforcers?! That means––
Damnit, I can see them too now! Where these guys go, the Arbiters follow!
Enforcer IX: Exploration... So, the Hermit really is the one behind all this.
They've got U-556 surrounded! Even a META doesn't stand a chance against this many of 'em!
During the early stages of METAmorphosis, it's easy to be intoxicated by that newfound power.
It happened to me, and I suspect it's happening to her.
In this state of recklessness, she's likely to fight to the death if backed into a corner. We have to go after her right now, Bismarck!
Jade, Zuikaku, give us aerial support while we close in!
Understood! Scrambling planes now!
KABOOOM!
More enemies? Dammit...
Of course they've got aircraft... Piss off, all of you!
KABOOOM!
Hey! U-556 is shooting at my aircraft!
What's she doing? Did her memories get wiped by METAmorphosis or something?
That's not the issue. She doesn't seem to have the ability to distinguish friend from foe at the moment. The more aggressively we intervene, the more likely she'll see us as enemies.
What do we do then? We can't just watch while she gets blasted to pieces!
I have an idea, but I'll need you all to help.
I'll make a beeline to U-556.
In the meantime, create a diversion and try to draw the Sirens away from us.
Ulrich, can you take command?
Sure. You be careful now.
I'll be fine. Good luck.
Alright, listen up! On my mark, fire three volleys at the Sirens and take heat off of Bismarck and U-556!
Once they're on us, we'll spread out away from 'em!
Remember, our job is just to keep them busy! Buy Bismarck enough time to get U-556 to safety!
Roger!
Okay! Go!

Return and Rebirth.say: Mirror Sea - α phase
Thanks to the team's coordinated efforts, nearly all Sirens had been lured away from U-556.
Now only a few remained at the center of the battlefield – Bismarck, U-556, and a few Enforcers who were dead-set on fighting despite their injuries.
ROOOAAAR!
With a simple order to her rigging, Bismarck forced the Enforcers to flee.
You're the last one standing in my way...
Fine, I'll make this quick!
My Lord needs me! I have no time to waste on you!
U-556 mounted her rigging and rushed straight towards Bismarck.
ROOOAAGHH!
The giant mechanical dragon moved on its own accord, forming a shield with its wings to halt the submarine's attack.
Her attack neutralized, U-556 dove back beneath the waters.
Enough, Geryon. Get back.
Good boy. Let me handle this.
At Bismarck's command, the steel beast reluctantly stood down.
She, too, took a step back, and that's when the water under her feet shook.
You're wide open! It's oveeer!
U-556 reemerged, her weapons pointed straight at Bismarck.
Bismarck answered the attack with open arms, embracing her dear friend.
You're right. It's over now.
The girl had a nightmare. A lonesome, fading light, just barely illuminating the figure of a person important to her sinking deeper into the depths.
Lord Bismaaarck!
Hang in there! I'm coming for you!
Her engines sputtered. She had already pushed them beyond their breaking point, and they threatened to give out at any moment.
Enemy after enemy emerged from the dark. A black mist congealed around her, becoming thicker and thicker. The currents seemed to be pushing her back towards the surface.
Where are all these jerks coming from?!
Ugh... Lord Bismarck! Just wait for me!
I promised I'd bring you back home even if I have to tow you!
They just won't stop coming... I...
...Get the hell out of my way!
She shot at the shadows, clawed at them, tore at them with her teeth, but it was no use.
Every time she destroyed them, they would form again between her and her sinking friend, as if mocking her.
Despite her best efforts, her slender fingertips could never reach.
Lord Bismarck... Wait... Hang on!
I just need to deal with these things... Then I'll be right there!
Just wait for me... Please...
Tears clouded her eyes. Her Lord sank deeper and deeper, until nothing but darkness remained.
Everything is fine now, U-556.
From the darkness, a voice called out to the little girl.
She gathered her courage and opened her eyes.
The dim light gradually became brighter, revealing the source of the voice.
There she was. The person she couldn't save in her nightmares.
Lord Bismarck...? You're... not dead?
No. You saved me, don't you remember?
Oh, yeah... I remember now... Thank goodness I wasn't too late...
You know, I had the worst nightmare just now...
You were sinking, and no matter how hard I tried, I couldn't reach you...
That's all it was. A nightmare. Look, I'm here with you now, alive and well.
Yeah... You're here with me, and...
We're... underwater?!
Haha. That we are, my little Parzival.
Come on. It's time to wake up.
Some time later...
Bismarck, we're done on our end.
And... looks like you've taken care of business as well.
Ulrich! Are you okay?!
I should be asking you that. How are you holding up?
Hey! Hey! Do you recognize me now?
Yeah... You're Jade.
And me? Remember my name?
Hi, Otto!
Phew... At least she's come back to her senses.
But, she still doesn't look like she used to.
Seems like we were too late after all.
No. Let's not jump to conclusions.

I used Geryon to administer some emergency treatment, but this corruption seems to be a lot more persistent and hasn't disappeared in its entirety. I fear we'll need to confront the one behind all this first.
It's totally fine, Lord Bismarck! I dig my new look!
Plus, I feel like I get stronger the more I fight. I can protect you better than ever before!
U-556... Unless absolutely necessary, I don't want you fighting at all until we return to the Floating Fortress.
The power you feel now is the same mistake I once made – the power of the Black Cube. Don't use this power lightly, or you'll only worsen your condition.
Wh-what?! Really?
I mean... If you're sure, then I'll do as you say!
Well... Happy ending, I guess? Everyone's safe and sound, despite everything.
Now all that's left is to find a way out of here.
The girls turned their eyes towards the Tower of Midgard and the beacon of light pouring from its gate.
Let's get going. There's only one way home.

Upside-Down.say: The girls once more returned to the Tower of Midgard and passed through the gate.
However, it didn't lead to where they hoped...
Thiiis... doesn't look like the Fortress.
They all looked around. While a few details had changed, they were, without a doubt, still trapped in the perplexing Mirror Sea.
Beyond the red sea expanding again, it's all the same...
(What will happen if it expands to cover everything? Nothing good, I imagine...)
Wait... What's that in the sky?
It almost looks like... a city.
Above them, a city full of high-rises was suspended upside-down in the starless sky.
Two helix-like pathways spiraled through the city, reaching down towards the Tower of Midgard's gate.
An entity that should not exist, yet somehow felt hauntingly familiar.
I keep rubbing my eyes, but it's not going away!
That's no illusion, silly!
It's flooding me with pings and rendering my radar completely useless!
The tallest building... Why does it seem like I've seen it before?
Have you? Where?
I can't quite tell from here. I need to get a bit closer.
Gneisenau, come along, will you? You too, Zuikaku. Might need your planes.
You should use the elevator to get up to the viewing platform.
It's the highest point on the tower and should give you a better view.
Heads up! I've spotted a Siren fleet within range of the facility!
...Huh, that's odd. They appear coordinated, yet they're not attacking us.
Wait, they seem to be fighting "something"...
What do you mean by that?
I... I can't get clear visuals on it all. It's like... my recon planes can't focus in on it at all!
The shape is so vague I can't even tell if it's a plane or a person...
It's almost as if it's wrapped in some sort of living fog... Solid, yet not really.
That doesn't tell us anything at all. Can you try taking a picture of it?
It's worth a shot, but don't expect anything.
A vaguely-shaped mass of mist... You sure that's not a ghost?
(An unidentifiable "something"... That sounds similar to what Queen Elizabeth encountered when Bon Homme Richard pursued her.)
Alright, we'll just have to get closer. I'll send in Regina.
("Please fight until the last light has faded"...)
Lord Bismarck, do you think we should all split up?
Yes. You can come with me and search the control center for potential leads.
Ulrich, Zuikaku, Gneisenau, head up to the observation deck and see what you can figure out about the city in the sky.
That's what I was gonna do anyway.
Regensburg, Otto, Jade, stand guard outside the facility and keep an eye on the Sirens and this unidentified enemy.
Whatever you do, don't intervene, and don't get too close.
Let the two sides wear each other out.
Understood.
And remember, stay vigilant at all times.
Our comms should still work at this range. Report anything you find.

Observation Deck.say: Mirror Sea - α phase, tower observation deck
Whoooa! The view from up here is amazing!
Is it this good up on the original Tower of Midgard as well?
Do you mean the Floating Fortress's control tower?
The view's not too bad, but we normally don't let unauthorized personnel onto the deck.
Dang. It's a great place to do recon from...
I mean, heck, you can make out every detail of that massive city up here.
Speaking of, now that you've had a closer look, does anything jump out to you?
Sort of.
It doesn't really match the architectural style of the Sirens.
Yeah. Now that you mention it, it almost looks like something... humans would build.
It doesn't mean much when they've copied us time and time again, but that's not important. Take a look at that huge tower in the middle.
In the dead center? Hmm, yeah, that one doesn't look anything like the other buildings surrounding it.
If you ask me, it looks more like...
The tower we saw in Compiler's mainframe when we took the fight to her.
I thought the same thing! Does that mean... this Mirror Sea might be another Siren's mainframe?
It's a possibility.
I'm thinking... Gneisenau said that The Hermit is behind the attack on the Fortress.
There's no way this tower is supposed to be her mainframe, right? Ahaha...
Your guess is as good as mine. None of us have ever seen an Arbiter's mainframe with our own eyes.
Not to mention, it would be immensely illogical.
Their mainframe allows them to essentially come back to life endlessly.
I can't think of any reason they'd willingly expose themselves like this.
Unless, they're trying to bait us into attacking it?
Highly unlikely. There's no way in hell we'd ever have the firepower to assault an Arbiter mainframe head-on.
If she wanted to get rid of us, she could easily overwhelm us with her vessels.
No, you're right. Why waste time on setting an elaborate trap when she could get rid of us in a fraction of the time?
Perhaps her goal is to force us inside the city, then.
I could see her thinking that we'd feel pressured to look for a place to flee the red sea once it starts closing in on us.
This city in the sky would be the obvious option in such a scenario.
Especially if she provided the means for us to reach it...
She wants to corner us for some reason, is what you're saying?
Still seems easier to just kill us and be done with it. One thing's for sure – we're in no condition to take on an Arbiter or a mainframe right now.
I say we head back. Let's ask Bismarck what we should do next.

Vision - Bismarck.say: Mirror Sea - α phase, control center
So this is where you found Ulrich, trapped in a nightmare...
Are you sure it's a good idea to use the dome projector, Lord Bismarck?
It's the quickest way to get a grasp on what this place really is.
All you need to do is keep an eye on me. If something happens to me, hit me with a dummy round.
O-okay! I'll try my best not to hurt you with it!
Heh. That would be preferable, yes.
Bismarck deployed her rigging and started to divert resources into anti-corruption measures as before, getting ready to place her hand on the dome's surface.
Instantly, her vision distorted like ripples moving across a lake's surface.
When she opened her eyes again, she once more found herself in that burning graveyard, with Friedrich collapsed on the ground next to her.
This was Ulrich's deepest fear, the root of her nightmares.
But I was the one to touch the dome this time, not her.
She rose to her feet, called her rigging, and looked around the area.
The metallic dragon slowly rose into the sky, conveying large amounts of information to Bismarck in real time.
It did not take long for her to locate the "edge" of the nightmare.
Beyond the edge of the burning graveyard was a thick wall of inscrutable black fog. Even one glance at it was enough to make one's stomach turn.
Through Geryon's eyes, however, she could catch a glimpse beyond the fog – of something moving, stirring.
What is that...?! Geryon, get closer! Blow away that fog!
ROOOAAAR!
Geryon let loose a piercing roar, the shockwave blowing away the black fog just enough to expose what lay below – a jet-black aircraft, with a red core that glistened like a sinister eye.
At the exact same moment the fog parted, a flash of light appeared on the horizon for just a moment.
Is it... beckoning me closer?
No, that light wasn't meant for me... It's what led Ulrich to this Mirror Sea!
KABOOOM!
ROOOAAAR!
An ambush?! Geryon, come back! Defensive position!
The red eye fixed on her seemed to glare harshly, and patterns of light soon appeared one after another across the mangled wreckages of the mass-produced ships.
They lurched to life, aiming their weapons at Bismarck.
Is that strange plane... controlling these Siren husks?
Setting secondary guns to automatic fire. Geryon, use your main battery to take out that command unit!
ROOOAAAR!
The steel dragon flapped its wings and set sights on the Siren aircraft.
Let's see what's behind that mask of yours!
The instant her cannons fired, Bismarck was abruptly whisked away back to reality.
Lord Bismarck! Wake up!
What happened? Did I start acting up?
No, you were fine! Jade warned us that a humanoid Siren is coming this way, and fast!
She scrambled her aircraft to intercept, but they were all shot down immediately!
And that's not the worst part! Zuikaku said the enemy is The Hermit herself!
...Sorry for waking you up, but I had to, or else everyone would be in danger!
(I was this close to finding something out, too... But, we'll need to deal with this threat before I can try again.)
Thanks, U-556. Come with me, but don't attack unless I order you to.
Okay! I promise I won't!

Vision - Zuikaku.say: Before reaching the gate, Zuikaku remembered a familiar scene from another Mirror Sea.
Huh? Is this the Sanctuary? Why am I here?
Remember what I said? About keeping your promise?
And then I died, just like that.
Shoukaku...?
No, this isn't right. The you I saw in the Sanctuary wasn't...
Real? Are you sure?
Are you sure your memory is correct?
Actually, never mind. Come closer, will you?
You're not my sister. Who are you?
Come to me, Zuikaku... I've missed you~
Bullcrap! I know what you're up to!
This is just a nightmare, and you're the one who planted those hallucinations in Akagi and Kaga!
Oh, how I wish you'd listen to your sister... Heehee...
Shoukaku's phantom disintegrated into a black matter which spread in all directions to engulf Zuikaku.
You're gonna pay for impersonating my sis!
Hiyaaah!
Zuikaku drew her blade. In a flash of light, her shikigami aircraft materialized and effortlessly tore through the black substance.
Or she thought. The bits of matter started crawling towards each other, forming once more into several figures.
Looks like you're not gonna let me walk out of this nightmare, huh?
But don't think you can break my will just by outnumbering me.
We're in the Sanctuary after all, and this is my home turf! Come at me, and do your worst!
Despite her confident retorts, Zuikaku tightly gripped the hilt of her sword.
The sound of windchimes rang lightly in her ears, followed by the rhythmic, almost serene crashing of waves.
The next thing she knew, she stood in front of a procession of torii gates leading into the distance.
Huh? The vision changed? What is it this time?
Whew... Need to stop, breathe, and think for a bit.
The first one was in the Sanctuary with an impostor of Shoukaku, and she suddenly split into a bunch of shadows...
And then... Yeah, I heard the sound of windchimes, and then I was here.
But that doesn't tell me a thing about where I am.
As she looked around, she once again heard the sound of windchimes from further ahead of her, up a series of stairs.
She looked up the stairs and saw the fuzzy figure of a person.
She squinted, and as the figure slowly came into focus, she suddenly felt a wave of nostalgia, warmth, and safety wash over her.
Wait! You're...
But as soon as she reached out, she was pulled from her vision by the loud explosion.
What's wrong? Why'd you stop moving all of a sudden?
We're not at the gate yet. It's a bit further up ahead.
Oh, uh, I'm fine! I remembered a weird dream from a while ago, that's all.
Ah, sorry about that. I had to do something, and didn't have any other method on hand.
It's fine, don't apologize! You only did what you had to do to wake me up, and it's not like I got hurt or anything.
Anyway, sorry for wasting time! Let's catch up with the others!

Inversion.say: Mirror Sea - ω phase
The gate vibrated as it came to life once more.
Contrary to Ulrich's expectations, however, it was not the city in the sky that awaited on the other end.
We didn't get to the city or the mainframe, huh...
My assumptions from before were a bit optimistic after all.
Hey, see that around us? The red and blue zones in the sea have swapped places!
And that's not all. The Tower of Midgard seems to be receiving that beam of light instead of emitting it!
Everyone started surveying their surroundings. Indeed, everything around them had changed drastically.
The city in the sky was gone without a trace, and the celestial bodies were back in their proper places.
And, from somewhere up on high, a pillar of light beamed endlessly into the heart of the Tower of Midgard, as if everything had been inverted.
Seems like we were thrown back out between the red and blue zones, just like before.
Except this time, we'd have to cross the red zone to get back to the tower.
Bismarck, I believe we should confirm the properties of the water before we proceed.
I agree. It would be ideal to collect samples from a few locations to assess how dangerous it is.
Jade, is it possible to do that using your aircraft?
It should be. I'll give it a try, at least.
Everyone kept their eyes on Jade's plane as it skimmed delicately over the surface of the foreboding water with a container in tow.
What the hell? You can even do THAT with your aircraft?
What can I say? Good girls practice their technique~
Some time later...
Ulrich rocked the test tubes containing water samples from both the red and blue zones, analyzing them through the light coming from the Tower of Midgard.
Clear and colorless. There's nothing unusual about either of 'em.
They're both regular saltwater.
Wait, what? Then how come one looks red and the other doesn't?
Based on what I've seen, I have reason to believe that this Mirror Sea has multiple "states."
Think about it. The column of light, the sea changing whenever we use the gate, and the sky and sea being reversed.
What causes all of these phenomena are basically sets of "rules" imposed by the Mirror Sea.
In other words, the water here has no inherent properties other than what the current set of rules dictates.
...Which is why I have two ordinary test tubes of sea water in my hands now.
Lemme get this straight... This place is basically "projecting" colors onto the sea?
That's a simplified way of understanding it. Except instead of projecting a color, the Mirror Sea is capable of projecting properties.
Woah, that's pretty crazy... How big of a projector would you need to do something on that scale?
But I guess the more important question is, are these properties you're referring to dangerous?
If I remember properly, Gneisenau said something about the red area seeming to be dangerous?
Yes, I did feel META corruption coming from the red zone... But nothing from these test tubes.
It certainly lends credence to your theory, but I can't say I've seen anything like this before.
It wouldn't surprise me if this Mirror Sea is projecting information onto its surrounding environment, but it's still just a theory at this point.
That's pretty crazy, but it does sound like something the Sirens would be able to pull off!
Take it with a grain of salt for now. We'll need to gather more evidence before we can make any sort of conclusive claims.
Then, uh, the only thing we can do is move forward, right?
Yes. Besides, we can't just sit around here forever doing nothing. We have to return to the Tower of Midgard one way or another.
Uh oh, Bismarck! I'm detecting some enemy signals from right behind us!
They appear to be the same type as the ones we encountered when we first got here! And it's a whole army of them!
Awfully convenient timing. Almost as if they're trying to force us into the red zone.
If we're gonna have to fight them anyway, we might as well engage them on our own terms!
Agreed. Our resources are limited, so we should avoid fighting battles of attrition.
Let's head straight for the tower. We should be far away enough to avoid the bulk of their fleet.
Geryon and I will do what we can to keep everyone safe, but do act quickly and decisively.
Time is of the essence! Let's get going!

Traces.say: KABOOOM!
The girls sailed forward as quickly as possible while moving in a tight formation, Bismarck and Geryon acting as sword and shield for the fleet.
Despite the palpable danger of the red zone, the group was able to traverse through it without losing combat effectiveness.
After losing a significant chunk of their pursuing forces, the Sirens began to fade from sight, signaling an end to the offensive.
My, my. Autonomous riggings sure look frightening, but aren't you the goodest boy ever~
Bismarck's rigging cocked its three heads, unable to understand what Jade meant.
A rigging's personality depends on its wielder... Hm?
Sensing something, Bismarck quietly placed her hand on her rigging.
(Traces of META activity...?)
Communicating with her rigging was fairly simple, as it was like her second half. She quickly noticed a response from her beacon tracker.
Is something the matter, Bismarck?
Looks like we're not the only ones out here.
Another META ship has been active here.
What?
Everyone instinctively turned their eyes towards Gneisenau.
Please, there's no way it's me. Surely you don't think Bismarck would bring up this subject as a joke?
No, you're right.
Wouldn't you have sensed something, though? You're a META yourself, and you keep talking about your instincts.
Unfortunately, this entire sector seems to have traces of META corruption as far as I'm concerned, so I'm the least useful person here in that regard.
Besides, we all have our own unique signals, so I have no way of recognizing someone I don't know.
You have those fancy beacon trackers, don't you? I reckon you'll have better luck using those than asking me for help.
...Uhh, about that.
This was kind of an emergency operation, and META beacon trackers don't exactly come cheap...
Come to think of it, Bismarck, how'd you find this META in the first place?
I installed a beacon tracker on Geryon before we departed. I've already cross-checked the signal with our database.
Whoever it is, I can safely say we've never encountered them before.
Huh? You baked a beacon tracker into Geryon on top of all the insane stuff he already can do?
Well, it's not that inconceivable. Geryon was specifically designed with combatting META corruption in mind, so a beacon tracker would naturally be part of his core functions.
Argh... I'm so JEALOUS... But also so impressed at the same time!! Write that down, Regina!
An unknown quantity, huh...
Think she might be connected with that unknown entity we faced earlier?
Huh? Can you create those black shockwaves as well?
No, that's not what I meant.
It's just... I think I saw something like it before.
I once had a friend who was undergoing METAmorphosis... She was determined to fight until her final breath.
Oh... Sorry, I didn't mean to bring back tragic memories.
It's alright. Few can put up a fight against that kind of power.
In any case, if these energy traces are still fresh, the person behind it should still be nearby. And, expect her to wield tremendous power.
Strong, by META standards? Think it might be one of the Ashes?
One of the Ashes could certainly fit the bill.
Unlike us, they don't care about collateral damage. They would have no issue with razing the whole tower should it stand in their way.
(If she's anything like Scharnhorst, there's a good chance we can negotiate with her.)
(Should worse come to worst and we're forced to fight an Arbiter, she could be the ace up our sleeve.)
(Hell, maybe she even knows where Friedrich's gone off to.)
(I know what I saw in that hallucination wasn't real, but I still want to know...)
In hindsight, it makes sense. We probably weren't the unknown enemy's intended targets.
Whoever this META is, we should look for her.
I'll do it. Just give me the order.
If she's from the Ashes, she should sympathize with the situation we're in!
Hm? Ah, yes.
Whether she ultimately ends up being a friend or a foe, this is a lead we at least should pursue.
But first, we need to make sure we're in a safe area – head for the Tower of Midgard's control room!

The Hourglass.say: Mirror Sea - ω phase
Lord Bismarck, wouldn't it be smart to patrol the area?
No need. I'll have my hands full with touching up Regensburg's rigging.
In the meantime, I want to discuss the Mirror Sea's structure with someone. Will you stay?
Of course! I'd do anything if it helps you out!
Heheh. Alright, then I'd like you to tell me what you think this place is.
What it is? Uhhhh... Hmmmm...
Don't think too hard. Just tell me the first thing that comes to mind.
In that case... A loop? Maybe an hourglass?
Yeah, I think it's like an hourglass!
An hourglass?
A really, reeeally big one.
Like, you know how at first, everything was red other than the area around the Tower?
And every time we used the gate, we ended up in the same place, but the red sea was getting closer...
Then, when we used it again after seeing the upside-down city, the red and blue were reversed!
So I think the Tower is the middle of the hourglass, and the gate is the passage of time.
The sea starts out blue, then gradually becomes red...
And when it's all turned red, the hourglass is flipped, turning red sea back into blue sea...
I've got it! If the red sea is sand, then us passing through the gate is what lets it fall through!
I mean, look at the Tower. Its light beam has changed from blue to red. I think that indicates what's on the other side of the hourglass!
And maybe the reason we saw the upside-down city is because we were standing on top of the sand!
This Mirror Sea is an hourglass, and the red sea represents sand falling down...
Using the gate only transports us through time, not space, during which sand falls down, expanding the red sea.
Hang on, that reminds me...
Yeah, we were looking for Ulrich when we first arrived, and there was no red sea here at all.
Oh, uh, you were sleepwalking at the time, so it's natural you wouldn't remember it.
While I can't be 100% sure, I think Jade was the first to observe the red sea.
(All I did remember from my episode is going through the gate and heading straight for the control center... No, wait, there's one more thing.)
Those giant cubes. I don't remember them being there.
Huh? That's weird. Gneisenau and I definitely saw them when we first got here.
If what Ulrich said is true, and the cubes aren't based on some kind of timetable, we must've done something to cause them to appear.
(Ulrich said that when she first arrived, there was no sign of the red sea, or those floating cubes.)
(After Ulrich activated the tower, Zuikaku and Gneisenau came through and saw the floating cubes, but not the red sea.)
(By the time I arrived, the red sea was already present, and with each activation of the tower, the red area grew more and more.)
(When we saw the upside-down city, that must've been when all the "sand" had passed through one phase.)
(Then, there's the corrosion effect of the red sea, the METAmorphosis phenomenon, and the unknown enemies that appear from the red waters...)
(But if we assume that this Mirror Sea houses a Siren Arbiter's mainframe, why would those things be here? Unless...)
...Oh my god!
What's wrong, Lord Bismarck? You look pale!
I think my guess might be wrong after all...
Ugh... Sorry, I didn't mean to make you worry.
I'm working with a number of assumptions here, but it's best if we act quickly.
Come with me, U-556. We're going back to the control center.
Wh-what is that?
In place of the previous vision of Friedrich, the dome in the central control room displayed a new, rather ill-boding image.
All of Iron Blood has been defeated?!
Heaps of wreckages floating in a burning sea. Among them were the girls of Iron Blood, including Bismarck's sister, Tirpitz.
Calm down. It's not real.
(My corrosion sensor is responding, though. This place really does connect to the throat of the hourglass.)
BEEP BEEP BEEP!
Bismarck, we've finished surveying the nearby waters. There seems to be nothing of note here.
The other META's trail also seems to have vanished, I'm afraid.
Don't draw conclusions just yet, Gneisenau. We're bound to find a lead on her if we keep looking.
I admire your drive, but pursuing a wild goose chase will only squander our finite resources.
If the META is here, we'll run into her sooner or later. If she's already left, we'll gain nothing by searching for her.
...Alright. We'll head back to the Tower.
Meet me at the gate. I have a theory I'd like to prove.
Are you sure about this, Lord Bismarck?
It'll be fine. The corruption in the control center is dormant for now.
Now, as for my theory... Use your communicator to relay a message to everyone.
"I strongly believe this Mirror Sea is structured like an hourglass."
"We are going to test if the gate advances the passage of time and makes the red sea, symbolizing sand, expand or not."
"If the red sea does not expand, or if it thins out, that means we're on the top side of the hourglass."
Ah! So once all the sand is gone, we can safely investigate the city at the hourglass' throat!
I've not made my mind on that just yet. We'd need to find a way to get there, too.
As I was saying... "Conversely, if the red sea does expand, that means we are on the bottom side."
"We must find a way out of this place as soon as possible, before the corruption bears down on us."

The Corruption Comes.say: Mirror Sea - ω phase
The Tower of Midgard glowed brightly, and the party once again stepped through the gate.
Bismarck, the red zone is even bigger this time.
It even looks like we were dropped off farther away from the tower than before.
Does that mean we've basically moved backwards?
It's as if the sand falling from the top keeps pushing us farther and farther away.
Going off our working theory, yes.
So basically, the corrupting stuff is raining from the sky?
Not literally, but expect to see it manifest in the form of that black mist from before, or perhaps that unknown enemy.
ROOOAAAR!
Speak of the devil.
Yeah, my aircraft have spotted something too!
A swarm of indiscernable blobs are pouring out of the Tower! They're heading straight for us!
First they went for the Tower, now they're coming out of it... What a bother.
Karma must be mad at me for being such a good girl all the time~
What shall we do, Bismarck? Run for our lives?
(These things seem to be able to materialize hull-corroding attacks. I don't want to push my luck with them twice.)
(On the other hand, the corrosion here isn't as bad as I expected... Depending on how we fight them, may be able to gather some valuable data.)
Ulrich, take command. Bombard them to slow them down.
And what are you planning to do?
I have a theory I'd like to test. With me, Geryon!
ROOOAAAR!
Geryon's thrusters roared to life, and Bismarck broke formation to charge straight ahead.
Right into the mass of the writhing, amorphous foes.
Bismarck?! Wh-what is she thinking?!
She's the only one here who can neutralize their hull-corroding attacks.
Most likely, she intends on using this opportunity to analyze exactly what those enemies are, up close and personal.
Everyone! Give her the space she needs! Don't let those enemies overwhelm Bismarck!
Although the colossal size of Bismarck's rigging made her an easy target, she managed to approach the enemies without taking much damage at all thanks to her fleet's covering fire.
Without a moment to hesitate, she engaged anti-hull-corruption measures and closed in.
Even this close, I can't make out any details...
Their very being seemed to defy description.
Then let's see how you respond to THIS!
She stretched her hand out, palm aimed straight at the enemy.
It responded almost like a parasite that had found a new host, emerging from its old one. It turned into a single string of mist before Bismarck absorbed it with her hand.
It felt like nothing in her hand – like grasping smoke.
...Just as I thought. The sand isn't so tough.
Geryon, can you keep going?
ROOOAAAR!
Good boy. If you're worried about me, don't be.
I know to stop before I reach my limit.
ROOOAAAR...
(Simply shooting them should work if this is all they're made of. Time for the others to shine.)

Divergent Paths.say: Mirror Sea - ω phase
Bismarck, we've taken out most of those... things.
So, did you get the answer to your theory?
Unfortunately, no.
But, I didn't leave empty-handed.
What I did learn is that those things are not Sirens of any kind that we know of.
They appear more to be rough "projections" from some sort of entity, but it's as if our psyches are trying to reject them outright.
While it's possible we're not designed to be able to perceive them, I fear they might be beings that transcend our cognition...
Until we can shed light on their true form, I propose we call them "Shades" for now.
In any case, we should try to steer clear of them until we have a better idea of what we're up against.
Projections? You mentioned that before...
Yes. Think of them as the shadows cast off from light.
There's one more thing I learned, and it's where we are in the hourglass...
So in short, the farther the red sea expands, the likelier we are into those manifestations of corruption.
Damn those Sirens, their schemes just get more and more convoluted... Unless the corruption is somehow separate?
What even is this corruption you keep talking about? Is it the reason why we've been having hallucinations?
Very likely. The last battle all but confirmed it for me.
Corruption is advanced corrosion of the hull. In other words, the energy of METAmorphosis.
The corrosion likely started to spread when Ulrich activated the Tower of Midgard, and has grown with each subsequent activation.
When the corrosion reaches a critical threshold, those Shades from before start to appear.
So let me get this straight, the Shades are an external corruption affecting the Sirens as well? Is that why the Sirens are trying to fight them off as well?
That makes sense, which also explains why The Hermit's vessels were largely ignoring us earlier and focusing on the tower instead.
They're most likely trying to destroy it to sever the source of the corruption.
So now my question is, how'd the corruption get inside in the first place? If this really is an external attack on the Arbiter's mainframe, how is it ever going to reach that upside-down city?
That could have something to do with why we were brought here in the first place.
Our initial assessment - that this whole thing is a trap, appears to be correct. But it wasn't a trap laid by The Hermit.
The Arbiters themselves are being targeted.
Really? Do you think the Ashes are behind it? Maybe it has something to do with that unidentified META?
Unlikely. Even with all the tools in their arsenal, that kind of conceptual attack isn't something I'd associate with the Ashes.
It's still possible they're responsible, given that not all METAs share the same goal.
But, if they wanted to spread corruption through repeated use of the gate, they wouldn't need our help to do it.
I'm more inclined to believe that like us, they were also lured in here...
Wait, Bismarck... It just dawned on me - the nightmares originated in our Floating Fortress, right? In other words...
Whoever it is that launched this attack against an Arbiter mainframe... is somewhere out there in our world.
I'm afraid so. At least, their actions are disruptive enough to leave a lasting mark.
Damnit... We need to get back there this instant! We still have allies out there who don't know about the dangers we might be facing!
If there's anything you should've learned in my absence...
Is that Iron Blood will continue to move forward, with or without my will.
For now, we need to focus on escaping so that nobody else gets caught up in this plot.
The longer we take, the stronger the Shades will get...
But how on earth are we supposed to get out of here?
We cannot use the Tower of Midgard any longer.
Whoever set us up clearly factored our reliance upon the tower into their plans.
It's hard to say what will happen once this entire Mirror Sea becomes a red zone, given what we currently know about its dangers.
In any case, we need to keep moving, but perhaps not in the same way as before.
This whole time, we've been fixated on the Tower of Midgard as the singular notable destination here, giving us the illusion that there's only one route.
But, I also had Geryon searching for traces of the unknown META during the previous battle.
And believe it or not, there were no traces of META energy.
In other words, the unknown META took a completely different path from us that does not go through the Tower of Midgard.
Finding that path is the next crucial step we must take.
Are you certain? I haven't seen anything that could possibly house something like a gate, other than the tower.
So it seems, but remember where we found Zuikaku and Gneisenau. They weren't in there.
The two of them blacked out inside the control center, yet Bismarck's party found them somewhere else entirely.
Perhaps they were subjected to a hallucination and moved after losing consciousness, but that's all the more reason to investigate it.
Why does the dome projector in the control room display a specific image to everyone who sees it, while everyone else has their own individual nightmares?
What I'm inclined to believe... is that not all hallucinations come from the same source.
Perhaps, the hallucinations themselves are similar to the way things are projected across this Mirror Sea.
If the corruption can travel through the control room, it may also be a channel we can utilize.
At the very least, it's worth investigating.

A Way Through.say: Mirror Sea - ω phase
The control center is ahead, Ulrich. I'll need your help.
Gneisenau, take command while I'm gone. Secure the Tower of Midgard and make sure the control room is safe.
Me? But...
...Alright. I'll do as you say.
Tower of Midgard - Control center
The two stepped into the command center. The projector dome's surface still continued to reflect the annihilated Iron Blood fleet.
Is this why you kept everyone away?
Yes. Sometimes, even fiction is not meant for everyone's eyes.
Yeah... Even if we rationally know something to be false, it is far harder to avoid having our emotions compromised.
You brought me because I've already experienced this once, so it won't have the same effect on me, right?
That's not the only reason I brought you.
Remember the other nightmares you told me about before?
The black sun. The tower calling out to you. Friedrich lying dead in the water.
Out of those three, only two were purposely meant to spur you into action.
The third one with Friedrich is the odd one out. If anyone else here had seen it on the dome, they'd immediately dismiss it as fake.
And yet, it showed it to you. It even showed it to me.
It showed it... to you?
It did. When I had that same nightmare, I caught a glimpse of what's controlling it.
That's when something happened. As soon as I tried to attack it in the nightmare, that Arbiter vessel began attacking us in reality.
In other words... the nightmare on this screen is connected to the Mirror Sea, and...
That's right. In fact, what we see here might not be a "nightmare" at all, but rather a stable space that can be entered, exited, and manipulated.
Which brings me back to a conclusion both Zuikaku and Queen Elizabeth reached independently: Mirror Seas seem to be malleable through "conceptualization."
If this Mirror Sea shares a similar property, as long as we grasp this Mirror Sea's framework, we should be able to use the concept of an hourglass to direct us to the throat.
I'm going to try something. During this time, I'll have no way of knowing what is taking place around me.
Wake me up if we're under attack, or if I've been inside for too long.
Alright then. Don't get yourself killed.
Bismarck reached out to touch the screen, and the scenery in front of her rippled like the surface of a lake.
In an instant, the boundary between reality and dream blurred together once more.

Vision - ???.say: The air wafting above the ocean was still hot and rancid, irritating the nostrils.
Bismarck rose to her feet and slowly looked around her.
Geryon rumbled with a hint of mechanical sorrow, whipping up a gale with its steel wings to clear the surrounding smoke and flame.
There, in front of him, lay a lifeless phantasm in the image of Bismarck's sister.
Tirpitz...
One of the dragon rigging's three heads brushed up against her hand, as if telling her to snap out of it.
Don't worry. I know none of this is real.
I know it isn't, but still...
Bismarck took a deep breath, before turning around and mounting her rigging. Geryon's wings flapped and his engines fired, and the two gradually rose from the water's surface.
...Let's do what we came here for.
Clear the mist and show us the way forward!
ROOOAAAR!
The wall of black mist along the outer border of the nightmare receded before the dragon's storm.
I don't see the aircraft from before, but there indeed is a path forward...
Bismarck followed through the path into the mist opened by Geryon.
The deeper she went in, the more the boundaries of the sea seemed to expand, seemingly reaching a scale of unfathomable proportions.
After what felt like ages, a light finally appeared in front of her.
ROOAAR...
Sensing the significance of this discovery through his owner's excitement, Geryon let out a low growl and accelerated in the direction of the light.
This must be the way to the hourglass' throat!
Without a hint of hesitation, Bismarck rushed directly through the light.
–Not only in the nightmare, but also in reality.

Lingering Echo.say: When the blinding light faded, Bismarck opened her eyes once more to an apocalyptic hellscape.
Beneath dilapidated structures half-submerged beneath the water, she saw the lonesome figure of a person.
She was clad in religious attire, her back turned towards Bismarck. Her melancholic features were reflected onto the water by the ashen sun.
Scattered around her were a number of glistening, gemstone-like objects.
Grrrrrrrrr...
Who is that...?
Bismarck signaled for Geryon to stand down, approaching the girl alone.
Even as the girl's face came clearly into view, Bismarck's attention was instantly drawn to the objects around her.
Wisdom Cubes?!
A number of shattered Wisdom Cube fragments lay scattered around the girl, appearing like gemstones at a pawn shop from a distance.
*sob*... Why did this have to happen...
I was completely useless...
I'm sorry... I'm so sorry...
You were the ones fighting on the front lines, so why is it that I get to live on?
You were supposed to get better...
My job was to ensure that all of you survive...
But I failed, and now I'm the only one left...
What point is there to go on without you...?
No... Maybe there's still time...
I've gathered up this many pieces of you already...
I just need to put you back together in the right order!
I will bring you back, even if nothing of your old selves remain!
The muttering woman suddenly fell to her knees and started scrabbling together the glittering fragments as if possessed.
What's the matter? What happened here?!
The woman ignored her.
Though blood dripped from her hands, that didn't stop her search for more glimmers of hope.
(This is not a phantasm... She's real. Is she a shipgirl who's been trapped in this place?)
Excuse me. I need to talk to you.
Bismarck gestured to Geryon, and the dragon let out a roar in an attempt to rouse the girl.
Her expression cleared for a moment. Then, the girl blinked, and slowly turned to face Bismarck.
Her tragic expression changed to a look of surprise, if only for a moment.
...This was once one of the largest, most prosperous cities.
Now, it is nothing more than rubble beneath your feet.
Over there is NY Port. On the other side, is where some of the world's most-renown architecture once stood.
I never thought I'd see another shipgirl touring this place...
Pleased to meet you. I am repair ship Vestal.
I am battleship Bismarck of Iron Blood. I take it you're a META.
That I am, yes.
Why are you here?
I could ask you the same. What brought you to this place?
I'm trying to save my comrades.
Your comrades? Not yourself?
Vestal reached forward and gently placed her hand on Bismarck's rigging.
So that's why. You're unlike the others.
Her curiosity piqued, Vestal stood up and looked intently into Bismarck's eyes.
You're hanging in there, but you must know that it's only a matter of time.
...I know.
Shh. Just listen to my diagnosis for now.
The Wisdom Cube constituting your hull has undergone severe corrosion.
So severe, that there are no known means to restore it to its original state.
But, you are capable of shifting the concept of "Bismarck" into an external augmented hull, to avoid damage to your own Wisdom Cube.
Am I correct?
How do you know all that?
Simple. I, too, am not the same person I used to be.
It's an ingenious idea, but as long as you keep fighting, the corruption will continue to consume your hull.
No matter how cautious you are, it will only get worse.
Vestal gently caressed the steel beast.
Geryon silently lowered its head, like an animal comfortable with being stroked.
...What are you doing?
Geryon – a warrior, but also a kindhearted cattle herder. What a lovely name. His three concepts suit him perfectly, too.
I reinforced his frame a little to allow him to endure as much corruption as possible. Don't worry, this treatment is free.
(Not just that, you also made him run smoother...)
(Is this your unique power as a META?)
Now, I assume you have other questions for me.
Not to be rude, but have you realized that this place is an illusion?
Thanks to you. This is a vision produced by the Tower of Midgard, no?
I sensed something was wrong with the tower, so I came to investigate. Unfortunately, that's how I ended up here.
Good, we're on the same page then. How do you plan to get out?
I don't have a plan, at the moment.
I can't fix the distortion at the root of this Mirror Sea, much less break out on my own.
My allies would surely know what to do, but on my own, I'm helpless.
If I could just contact them, maybe they could come and rescue me.
But for the time being, we're on our own.
What if I offered to help you?
I see no reason to refuse.
But just so we're clear, I am not one of the Ashes. I overheard your conversation earlier, and apologize for the confusion.
Meaning, you're the unidentified META whose signal we picked up.
Indeed I am. I wish I could tell you where Friedrich is, but I do not know.
At least this saves us time in getting you up to speed. I'm guessing you know what's at the throat of the hourglass, too?
You mean the upside-down city? Did you see it as well?
In all its "glory." Do you what the tower in the middle of it is?
I suspect it's the Mirror Sea's control device.
With all due respect, why didn't you investigate it when you had the chance?
*sigh*... We only just figured out this place is structured like an hourglass. Back then, we didn't know it was important.
We were so focused on the Tower of Midgard, we assumed that was our key to getting out.
But, the tower itself is part of the trap, and using it only causes us to sink deeper into it.
And so, you went diving into hallucinations, looking for answers. Just like me.
Actually, I already developed a theory before coming here.
I could explain it, though the details might be lost on you if you're not up to date on the Sirens.
By all means, let's hear it.
Alright. To start with...
Using the power of concepts to move between dimensions, similar to the corruption...
I thought only Helena could do something like that...
Helena?
Oh, just thinking out loud. Your theory seems fairly sound.
If you can analyze the structure of an illusion, then the illusion itself can be considered a pathway of sorts.
Given the proper methods, it is not only consciousness that can pass through, but perhaps even matter.
The very thing that keeps us trapped is also our key to escape.
Yes. Well put.
You passed through two sets of visions on your way here, did you not?
Following the trail of visions may actually lead us to the hourglass' throat.
But first, would you please show me back to vision of your origin?
Of course. Geryon, send us back.

Virtual Construct.say: After the blinding light faded, a new illusion appeared in front of Bismarck.
This time, a group of shipgirls were breaching the enemy line to the peal of roaring cannons.
I've lost contact with Thüringen's squadron, Lady Friedrich! Things are looking dire!
We won't last much longer!
The time isn't right. You must keep fighting.
They're surrounding us!
Intercept them.
Here I thought you'd at least come prepared. What a joke.
Disappear.
One by one, the members of Friedrich's fleet were engulfed by continuous, overwhelming artillery fire.
Have you been shaken by this vision?
No, I'm thinking... About how all these visions seem to be connected.
...Were these visions truly fabricated, or are they based on reality?
It's not impossible. Originally, these visions were just a way for Sirens to store information.
Some of their contents are from simulations run by the mainframe, while others are records from actual events in the experimental field.
Later on, the Sirens would go on to weaponize these visions just as they did with all technology at their disposal, much like the Mirror Seas.
Or... They could be things that happened in other branches.
A keen observation. I didn't expect you to ask me a question from this angle.
What if I were to tell you, "yes"?
...All the more reason not to dwell on it.
Getting back on topic... Assuming these visions are all connected through temporal continuity, I suspect that we're seeing them in the reverse order.
So, if my theory about traveling through concepts is correct, following these visions should lead us to their source.
Indeed. We might already know the way to move backwards.
You mentioned something earlier about a black aircraft beyond the mist, yes?
I think I have an idea of where to find it.
If I remember right, it should be... this way. Let's go, Geryon.
Bismarck engaged her engines and followed the vision's foggy boundary line.
ROOOAAAR!
Geryon flapped its wings to create another blast of wind, its movements seemingly smoother and more flexible than before.
The obscuring veil parted to reveal multiple jet-black Siren aircraft. The instant they spotted Bismarck, they scattered and began to flee.
You're not getting away this time! Geryon!
...There's no need for that. I already know who those planes belong to.
They belong to an Arbiter... Arbiter: The Devil XV.
The Devil? Not The Hermit?
We're dealing with not one, but TWO Arbiters now?!
I'm afraid we'll need to gather more intel...
First, though, the way out of the vision is open. Let's get out of here!
After passing through the light again, Bismarck once again found herself in front of the Tower of Midgard and the inverted city in the sky.
...This isn't where we're supposed to be. I must've taken a wrong turn.
Are we still dreaming? This doesn't feel real to me...
We're not. I can confidently say that this is reality.
(But why did we end up here? Outside interference? Or is the corruption affecting Bismarck's hull?)
Don't worry about your allies. We'll meet up with them soon.
...There it is. This place is where The Hermit's mainframe is.
There's no mistaking it. The Mirror Sea, the upside-down city, and that massive tower...
That's it... We must've been sent here by someone who wants the mainframe destroyed.
Perhaps so. One thing is for sure – your presence here has made attacking the mainframe much easier.
...Oh! I've regained connection! I can talk with my allies again!
That must mean The Tower XVI still hasn't gained full control of this place.
After breathing a sigh of relief, Vestal punched a number and a code into her communicator.
Helena, can you hear me?
Vestal! Thank goodness you're alive.
Where are you right now? What's your status?
The good news is, I'm in the Mirror Sea that houses The Hermit's mainframe. As for the bad news... matters have gotten complicated.
Bismarck is here, and we spotted aircraft belonging to The Devil.
"The Devil" as in Arbiter XV, correct?
Yes. I don't know why she's here, but it's a very bad sign.
I'll send you all the intel I've gathered now while I still can. There's no telling how long this connection will remain stable.
Deep breaths, Vestal. Let me just... There, I got your intel.
Hmm? Now, that's an interesting quandary.
There's a whole Iron Blood fleet out there? How'd they get in?
Hang on, I'll send you some data of my own. It'll help you out of the situation you're in.
*huff*... *puff*... Okay. I received your data.
Wait, how is that possible? ...The Devil already has full knowledge of the Ashes' movements?!
We don't know for sure, but we have to work under the assumption that she does.
We've already sent a rescue team after you, but...
I'm afraid the place you're stuck in is impossible to infiltrate from the outside.
The only way I can help you is by hacking the Mirror Sea to your benefit.
The Hermit has a vessel stationed near her mainframe. Find and destroy it as soon as you can.
In the meantime, I'll do whatever I can.
...Okay. I'm on it.
I'm sorry about all this. I shouldn't have sent you there.
Don't apologize. I'll be okay.
Who was that?
Helena, our "ol' reliable."
I'm sure you have many questions, but let me cut to the chase.
First and foremost, Friedrich is alive and well. She and the Ashes are currently engaging The Hermit.
While she was away, another Arbiter, The Devil, infiltrated her mainframe.
As for why she'd do that, I cannot say. All I know is, she's causing damage to The Hermit while the latter is pinned down by the Ashes.
Causing damage to her?
Are you saying there's infighting between the Arbiters?
No, not infighting. All the Arbiters are Zero's subprograms. They would never turn on each other in the conventional sense.
But that may change if there's a special experiment involved. In any case, this is the first time we've ever witnessed something resembling conflict between Arbiter programs.
Whatever her reason, it works in our favor. Won't we be rid of The Hermit for good if her mainframe is destroyed?
If only it were that simple. Should that be the case, the Arbiters would've been wiped out long ago.
This is but one of many mainframes, and we have no way of knowing how many she has, or where they are hidden.
However... If you're only interested in obtaining or tampering with certain pieces of information, invading the mainframe certainly is the most efficient method.
At least, that's what Helena suspects.
Then, is The Devil behind all of this? The nightmares, the weaponization of hull corrosion, and the vision of Friedrich we saw just before?
Actually, some of those things are parts of The Hermit's arsenal, which may signify that The Devil has begun to gain control of the mainframe.
As for the use of META corruption, this has only been observed in relation to The Devil.
Maybe that has something to do with The Devil's current condition...
But that's speculation, and even we can't say for sure one way or the other.
Ultimately, her presence here isn't entirely beneficial to us. If we were to bump heads with her, we stand no chance of winning.
Her vessels are a different story, though. We can defeat those if we work together.
That much I already know. We ran into one of The Hermit's vessels earlier and took care of it.
But, since she only sent a vessel, she mustn't be too alarmed by us being here.
Pardon? You fought one of The Hermit's vessels?
Yeah. Is that a problem?
Well, no... Like you said, it just means she doesn't consider you a serious threat.
At any rate, it's time we reunited with your allies. Let's get moving.
Alright. By the way, what you said about my rigging earlier...
I know. I won't tell them about it.

Critical Mass.say: Some time later, in the Tower of Midgard's control center...
...The hell?! Who are you?!
Forgive me, you couldn't have anticipated my arrival.
I am Vestal – though not the Vestal you know. Well met, Ulrich von Hutten.
She's the unknown META we were searching for before.
Ulrich, gather everyone. I'll explain what we're going to do next.
The Devil wants The Hermit's power, so she called us here and crippled the mainframe using her corruption...
And to get out of here, we'll need Helena META to hack the mainframe for a while?
Seems the Arbiters are so despicable they've started fighting each other.
Hey, just be happy we didn't have to duke it out with one of the kingpins.
In fact, it doesn't seem like either of the two have plans on annihilating us.
As much as I don't like the idea of running with our tails between our legs, I'll do anything to get out of this place.
Yes, and since The Devil's attention is on seizing control of the Mirror Sea, she should leave us alone for the most part.
That said, I don't think we can just walk out. Her aircraft will still pose a threat to us.
Especially so, considering her arsenal of support aircraft is the most advanced out of any Arbiter – or Siren, period.
The Commander must have warned you about their capabilities.
Yeah. The information you provided will be useful, too.
The Devil must know we're here, seeing how far the red sea has expanded...
Our window of time to get out of here shrinks with every passing minute.
Speaking of which, Bismarck, the sea didn't expand while you were gone.
The gate didn't activate a single time. It was only just before you got back that the red sea grew.
Either The Devil is trying to put pressure on us, or the corruption has reached critical mass.
Which means our time is running out. We need to get moving immediately.
We must defeat The Hermit's vessel before The Devil gains full control over the mainframe.
If we take too long... we will be trapped in here forever.
Now, let's access the database in the control center. It should take us to the hourglass' throat.
One quick thing before we do that. Could you examine this one?
Bismarck held U-556's hand and led her to Vestal.
Ah. U-556, yes? You told me about her.
(I sense the corrosion eating away her hull... But wait, it's not past the point of no return...)
Vestal felt a little confused seeing such a bright smile on a body so clearly tarnished by the corruption.
She suffered damage to her hull long ago, and the red sea's corrosion suddenly reopened and aggravated her old wounds.
I entered her vision and managed to cleanse the external corruption, but she's still in this form...
I see...
Luckily, her condition is still reversible. I should be able to cleanse the internal corruption as well.
Just give me a minute, please.
...Thank you so much.
Please, it's nothing. One important thing, though – she still needs to abstain from fighting for a while after I've treated her.
Alright.
Hey, hold on a sec!
Vestal, you know more than anyone else here about METAmorphosis, right?
I... suppose so.
Okay, then listen. We're still in danger and need as many of us fighting as possible.
I'm the Parzival of the Seas, and I'm duty-bound to protect Lord Bismarck as well as all my other friends!
If you cure me now and I'm forbidden from fighting, I'll only be dead weight!
So, could you just... put a bandaid on, but let me stay like this for a while longer?
You can fully cure me after this is all over!
Are you sure you want to do that?
(Even if she's willing to take a risk, there's a lot at stake here...)
(Worse comes to worst, she might lose herself again...)
Okay. I'll do what I can, but if you push yourself too far, there's no going back.
You need to promise that if Bismarck or I order you to stop fighting, you will do so at once. Is that clear?
Yeah! Promise!

Final Gamble.say: Following Bismarck's guidance, the girls all touched the control center's dome and were transported through the datastream.
They carefully traversed it to its terminus and arrived beneath the inverted city, only to discover that it looked nothing like it did before.
Oh no! What's wrong with this place? It looks totally corrupted!
Yeah, what's going on? Everything here is dyed red!
Are we too late after all?
If The Devil has seized full control, there's no point in going after The Hermit's vessel.
In that case, our only hope of ever escaping is to take on The Devil herself...
I fear that may be the case.
If only I could reach Helena and ask for help...
There's no going back to the control center now. It's do or die!
My planes have spotted The Hermit's vessel! She's not far from here!
Although... is it just me, or does she not look right?
Zuikaku put on display the picture her aircraft took.
The Arbiter vessel was glowing red and standing idly in the sea as though it was waiting on somebody.
The robotic yet intense drive its kin displayed the last time they fought one was completely gone.
Just to confirm, Sirens do not have Wisdom Cube-powered hulls, and thus cannot be affected by METAmorphosis. Is that correct?
Yes. In theory, Sirens should immune to the effects of corrosion.
While they do utilize Cube technology, they only use it as a power source. It's not integral to their design.
It may be possible to damage this power source, but that would have no bearing on the Siren's properties, let alone cause METAmorphosis.
Both you and the Commander said that The Devil isn't like other Arbiters.
So if this isn't the effects of corruption, what is it then?
Well, there's another theory about that... It basically holds that The Devil's erratic qualities are perfectly normal.
Just as their naming pattern implies, the Arbiters may have both upright and reversed forms.
Under this theory, she is simply exhibiting the reversed characteristics of her Major Arcana.
Of course, there's no proof one way or the other, and the theory has holes.
(She's caught between the ideas of being a person and being a weapon, much like us.)
In any case, this vessel is a wholly different beast to the last one you fought.
The Devil's support makes it many, many times more powerful than before.
The difference is so drastic, fighting this vessel would be like taking on two Arbiters at once.
(Worse yet, none of us here have fought a juiced-up Arbiter before...)
(If only I could reach Helena, or if Arizona was here with us...)
(Or better yet, if I was only a more capable fighter...)
(Enty... Give me courage...)
(Vestal looks like she's about to break down...) It's alright, Vestal! Let's think this through first!
What if The Devil hasn't actually seized full control over this place yet, and is actually bluffing?
If she's already seized full control, she wouldn't have any reason to care about us!
She's just trying to stall for time! We can win if we take her on here and now!
(Zuikaku...)
I... I think you're right.
The Devil is an incredibly cunning Arbiter. I certainly wouldn't put it past her to resort to deception...
I believe we can win. Let's take on this vessel!
That's what I like to hear.
Now it's our turn to go on the offensive!
Yes. I've had enough of dancing in the Sirens' palms.
Everyone! Commence the attack!

The Tower.say: The Mirror Sea began to change immediately following the destruction of The Hermit's vessel.
Massive metallic rings suddenly emerged from the red sea, surrounding the Tower of Midgard from all sides.
Meanwhile, in the sky, Bismarck began descending back down and saw the ring fuse itself to the big cubes floating above the surface.
In only a matter of moments, the ring and the cubes had merged into one single structure.
I assume this is the work of that friend Vestal spoke about.
She wields powers far beyond what I imagined...
Immediately upon landing, Bismarck was hit with an overpowering sense of dizziness that brought her to her knees.
Lord Bismarck! Are you okay?!
Stay back!
But... Lord Bismarck... Look at me.
U-556 came closer, despite Bismarck's clear order. Black mist was coiling around the little girl.
Umm... Looks like I got a bit too excited...
There are still Enforcers around... can you fight?
Sure!
...No. This isn't the time for that.
I am battleship Arizona. One of Vestal's friends.
Allow me to handle this.
The giant ring encasing the Tower of Midgard began glowing, rapidly increasing in intensity until everything was dyed a blinding white.
Little by little, the light dimmed until it was possible to see again.
What was that?! The red sea is completely gone!
The Sirens all went up in smoke too...
And that's not all...!
Bismarck! U-556! Are you alright?
Where... am I...?
Indeed, the red sea had disappeared entirely, as though by magic.
As was the noxious air signifying the presence of corruption.
U-556 and Bismarck regained their footing and listened to the META girls' conversation.
Arizona... You...
You used the tower? Why?
It was the only way to resolve the situation.
And Helena? Where is she?
She... has her hands full right now.
Never mind her. Shouldn't you tend to those two?
Tend to– Oh my god...
Vestal regained her bearings and turned her attention to Bismarck and U-556.
The black mist had all but completely enveloped them.
You've been exposed to extreme amounts of corrosion!
What happened?! What did you do?!
...You disabled your limiters, didn't you?!
Why would you do that?! You nearly got yourself killed!
Visibly shocked by the state they were in, Vestal rushed to their side.
You have to stay awake! Can you talk?!
Don't worry about me...
I took measures against the corrosion... It'll be fine...
My rigging will absorb it...
Focus on U-556... She's the one who needs help...
...If you say so. I'll take care of her.
U-556? Can you hear me?
It's so dark... Lord Bismarck... where are you...?
Is it... too late... for her...?
...No, it is not.
She can still be saved.
Vestal kneeled by U-556's side and opened her hand, manifesting a cube which glowed with a soft blue light.
Dozens more appeared shortly after, forming a loose circle that slowly swirled around her and U-556.
Little by little, the cubes absorbed the black mist enveloping U-556.
After being treated for a while, the pained look on her face eased while the black deposits gradually discolored the cubes.
CRACK...
The cube in Vestal's hand first turned a red color, then crumbled to pieces.
Her facial muscles contorted with pain.
*cough, cough*! Urgh...
Vestal! Are you alright?!
I'll be fine... I just need to rest for a while...
(Now we're square, and can part ways without debts to settle...)
Zuikaku offered Vestal a hand, but she politely shook her head and stood up on her own.
The next time eyes turned on U-556 again, she had reverted to her regular appearance. All external signs of the corrosion were gone.
Nngh...
She's finally back to normal!
Whoa, hey! I'm soaking wet right now! Hugging me just feels icky!
Give her some space. She needs to recover.
Right. My bad...
You had me worried sick, you know... I thought you'd never be the same again.
Well, now I am, so it's all good!
Seems everything turned out fine in the end.
I sent you the data. I trust you to take care of the aftermath.
Yes... It's all here. I'll take care of it.
Then this is farewell. Until next time...
So, this is it? It's over?
Yes. I got what I was looking for.
All you need to do now is activate the Tower of Midgard's gate one last time, and you should be sent back home.
...I still cannot believe the corrosion can be purged.
Although, I suppose doing so requires a sacrifice. It still has to go somewhere.
While your hull remains intact, the same cannot be said for those merged with it... Geryon will be out of commission for quite some time.
I suppose so. What do you plan to do next?
I'm guessing you're not thinking about coming with us.
Unfortunately, no. I have other things I must do.
Truth be told, I must ask you for a favor. Once you're back home, please see to it the Tower of Midgard in your world is destroyed.
It was the reason why this whole near-disaster happened in the first place.
Destroy it? Are you serious?
Do you know the ungodly amount of resources we poured into it?
Yes, I do.
I also know this is an extremely hard decision to make, but it must be done.
The Tower of Midgard's exact location is already out there now. Simply closing the gate won't prevent future incidents.
Leave it intact, and one day, the corrosion will come flooding through the gate and spread through your whole world.
If you don't want that to happen, you must destroy it.
...Very well. I swear the Tower of Midgard will be destroyed as soon as possible.
Good. You should get moving now, while the Arbiters have their eyes on me.
At the Mirror Sea's heart, the open gate shone with a bright blue light.
The massive machine rumbled and roared, presenting the girls with a way home.
Vestal, thank you for everythiiing!
With a final goodbye from U-556, the Iron Blood girls' long excursion was finally over.
I'm sure our paths will cross again.
A while after Vestal watched the group disappear in the light, the communicator in her hand started beeping.
...I wondered when you were going to call me.
...So, tell me, The Tower XVI...
What will you do now?

An Ending, and a....say: Floating Fortress - Area A1
Whew... It's good to be home again.
I wonder if Queen Elizabeth solved her Singularity issue.
Might as well call her and ask.
Bismarck opened up a communication channel.
She called up the queen, but the only thing on the other end was white noise.
Huh? That's strange...
Confused, she then attempted to get in touch with her comrades on the Iron Blood mainland, as well as those attending the World Expo.
But, again, the only thing she heard was white noise.
Is the Mirror Sea's jamming field still active? That's really odd...
Bismarck, I've finished my inspection.
How are the others holding up?
Just fine. They're all still sleeping like babies.
...Anything else that's wrong with them?
There's nothing wrong with them, medically speaking.
They're just sleeping. That's all.
Very well. Continue monitoring them.
As you say.
Bismarck gazed through the window in her office. The Tower of Midgard's light was fading.
They had promptly shut off the gate and deactivated the facility as soon as they got back.
It's supposed to be over. Everything should be back to normal, and yet...
Destroying the Tower of Midgard really is the only way, it seems.
She observed the silent tower whilst weighing its pros and cons in her head.
Ulrich, do you hear me?
Loud and clear. What's up?
Are you done inspecting the tower?
Still working on it. It's gonna take a lot of time if you want me to be thorough.
But, from what I can tell so far, it's back to working as normal.
– Bismarck... – Ulrich...
Ahah. We both had the same thought, huh?
...Are we really going to destroy it?
...Yes.
It has served its purpose. If Friedrich were here, she would agree that destroying it is the correct move.
But before we do so, download the logs saved on it.
Make backups of all the project files on it as well. Once that's done, demolish it.
Got it. I'm on it.

Capture.say: Somewhere, in an unknown location...
A sea dyed deep red. This battlefield had become a grave shipyard – the final resting place for dozens of smoking wreckages.
Friedrich lay on her back in the water, gazing silently into the distance.
Although her rigging had suffered terrible damage, the light of determination still glimmered in her eyes.
KABOOOM!
The Hermit fell to her knees, Enterprise META having dealt the final blow.
...You did good, keeping her busy.
No need to thank me.
We're done on our end. All of you, give me a status report.
This space has been successfully sealed. The Hermit cannot get help from other Arbiters.
